steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Walking takes a fixed 24 minutes. Taking the bus takes $w$ minutes of waiting plus 6 minutes of riding, for a total of $w + 6$ minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Walking is faster than the bus when the walking time is less than the bus total time: $24 \lt w + 6$, which rearranges to $w + 6 \gt 24$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • $w -6 \lt 24$ simplifies to $w \lt 30$. This is true for much of the 0–36 range and does not identify when walking is faster. Incorrect.
  • $w -6 \gt 24$ simplifies to $w \gt 30$, which comes from subtracting rather than adding the ride time. Incorrect.
  • $w + 6 \lt 24$ simplifies to $w \lt 18$. This says the bus is faster than walking, the opposite of what is asked. Incorrect.
  • The matching choice: $w + 6 \gt 24$ directly states that the total bus time exceeds the 24-minute walk, so walking is faster. D is correct.
